name: Prudhomme's Lemon Dill Chicken
recipeIngredient: Chicken breasts, boneless and skinless (8 pieces, 2 to 3 ounces each)
Artificial sweetener (2 packages, 1 gram each), optional
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine salt, dillweed, dried sweet basil leaves, black pepper, and white pepper in a small bowl.
2. Sprinkle the seasoning mix evenly over all surfaces of the chicken pieces. Rub the seasoning mix into the chicken well.
3. Dissolve half a teaspoon of cornstarch in ¼ cup apple juice. Set aside.
4. Preheat a heavy 10-inch skillet, preferably non-stick, over high heat to 350 degrees Fahrenheit for about 4 minutes.
5. Place 4 chicken breasts in the skillet and lower the heat to medium. Brown each side for at least 1 minute. Remove the chicken and brown another 4 pieces.
6. Return the heat to high. Stir in half a cup of stock, scraping the bottom of the skillet to remove any browned bits.
7. Add onions and the remaining seasoning mix. Stir. Cook until all liquid evaporates (3 to 4 minutes).
8. Stir in a quarter of lemon juice and scrape the bottom of the skillet again to remove any browned bits. Cook until liquid evaporates (3 to 4 minutes).
9. Add half a cup of apple juice, scraping the bottom and sides of the skillet. Cook until about half of the liquid has evaporated.
10. Stir in the remaining stock, lemon juice, and apple juice. Bring to a boil (takes about 2 to 3 minutes). Whisk in the cornstarch-apple juice mixture and return to a boil.
11. Return the chicken to the skillet, lower the heat to medium, and cook until fully done (about 4 to 5 minutes).
12. Turn off the heat. If desired, whisk in the artificial sweetener.
Makes 4 servings.
